file.content:
- [x] ✅ Severity tier toggle in check editor — three-threshold UI — optional warn/fail/critical inputs banding the unexpected-% (ADR 0016), blank = binary — [PR #203](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/203)

### Access & admin UI (3 tasks — 3/3 ✅)
- [x] ✅ Suite sharing panel — add / remove users, assign roles inline — **Share** button on the suite detail opens `SharePanel` (drawer): lists collaborators by name (from the enriched `ShareRead`) and, for owner/admin, adds/changes/removes them. Add picker is a debounced `GET /users/search` type-ahead filtering out the owner + already-shared users; permission is the view/edit/admin ladder. Gated on **`SuiteRead.my_permission`** — a new field stamping the caller's effective level (owner/admin/edit/view), populated on create/get/update + batched across the list via `suite_authz.effective_permissions` (one shares `IN`-query, no N+1) — so a non-owner admin gets the manage controls and a viewer a read-only list. Backend enablers landed first: **PR-E2** (`GET /users/search` behind a FastAPI-free `user_service` — case-insensitive email/display_name substring, 2-char min, limit≤50, LIKE-escaped — + `ShareRead` enriched with `email`/`display_name` via a joined read-only `Share.user`). 6 component tests (list-by-name, empty, search→grant with owner/existing filtered, change, revoke, read-only non-admin) + backend `my_permission` + `/users/search` tests. _(PR-E2 + PR-E3)_
- [x] ✅ Admin page — list all suites, all users, access overview — **workspace-admin control centre** shipped (backend + frontend, #289, closes the long-standing backend block). New **workspace-admin** authz primitive (config `WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS` allowlist, case-insensitive on `user.email` — generic identity axis, no Azure/Entra claim read; **no migration**) distinct from the per-suite `view < edit < admin < owner` ladder. Backend: `admin_service` (`list_all_suites` / `list_all_users` / `list_all_access`, deliberately **unscoped** — bypasses the owned-or-shared scoping `list_suites` applies) + `GET /admin/{suites,users,access}` gated by `require_workspace_admin` (real **403**); `/me` now exposes `is_workspace_admin`. Frontend: `/admin` page (Suites · Users · Access tabs) + `Forbidden` 403 page + admin-only nav item, gated on `/me`'s server-driven flag via a shared `MeProvider`/`useMe` (one `/me` fetch, reused by Profile; resets on sign-out). 23 tests (backend: scoping-bypass, counts, case-insensitive, non-admin 403, `/me` flag; frontend: forbidden gate, all three tabs, load error, MeProvider sign-out reset). _(This pulled the Week-7 prototype-adoption tasks "workspace-admin list-all endpoint" + "Admin control centre page" forward.)_
- [x] ✅ Suite export / import UI (download JSON, upload JSON) — **Export** button on the suite detail header downloads `<suite>.json` from `GET /suites/{id}/export` via a transient-anchor blob (`utils/download.ts` — `downloadJson` + `toFilenameStem`); **Import** button on the suites header opens `ImportSuiteDrawer` — drag/drop a `.json`, client-side parse + shape-check (fail-fast on non-suite/malformed JSON), pick a target connection, `POST /suites/import`. The parsed document is handed back **untouched** so thresholds round-trip exactly (string-or-number Decimal encoding never coerced). 11 tests (download lib: stem slug + blob/anchor/revoke; drawer: valid→import-with-faithful-document, non-suite/malformed-JSON rejection, connection-gated Import). _(PR-E1; backend ready from Week 3)_

### GX-Cloud-style UI redesign (added — not in original roadmap) (4 tasks — 4/4 ✅)
> Model the UI on [GX Cloud](https://greatexpectations.io/gx-cloud/): dedicated **full-page create flows** that **classify** what you're creating, keeping the lighter drawers for *edit*. Plan: `~/.claude/plans/fancy-moseying-raccoon.md`. Decided: in-app results page (Grafana deferred to an optional post-v1 ops add-on, never the per-suite authz path — ADR 0018 pending); full-page create + drawer edit; connections/checks redesign now (backends ready), results after the Week-5 run-enablement backend.
- [x] ✅ **Connections — dedicated, classified add page** — `/connections/new` splits the type picker into **Data sources** vs **Orchestration** sections (CLAUDE.md §4), then the spec-driven form; the list page is grouped the same way. `CONNECTION_KIND` is the single source for the split. Edit/re-auth/delete stay on the drawer. _(PR-A1; `ConnectionNew.tsx` + sectioned `Connections.tsx`)_
- [x] ✅ **Connections — `ConnectionDrawer` trimmed to edit-only** — create now lives on the page; the drawer drops the type/env Selects + secret + create branch, shows type/env read-only, and the list owns a simpler `editing: Connection | null` state. _(PR-A2)_
- [x] ✅ **Checks — suite selection as a route + categorized expectation picker** — `/suites/:suiteId` makes the selected suite deep-linkable and survives the check-editor round-trip; the catalog gains a `category` (Column values / Table shape) and the picker groups by it (antd optgroups). Reserved Freshness/Volume/Schema-drift categories (ADR 0012) land with the dedicated page. _(PR-B1)_
- [x] ✅ **Checks — dedicated `/suites/:suiteId/checks/new` page** — category → expectation → config/thresholds, with reserved monitor-kind categories (Freshness/Volume/Schema-drift, ADR 0012) shown disabled. Shared form logic extracted to `checkForm.ts` (conversions) + `checkFormFields.tsx` (field components), reused by the page and the now edit-only `CheckDrawer`. The natural home for the [#215](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/215) target table/path field. _(PR-B2; `CheckNew.tsx`)_

**Week 4 total: 26 / 26 ✅ — complete** _(connection manager UI complete (6/6); check editor core shipped + **dry-run preview** (PR-D1) + **column profiler panel** (PR-D2) + the datasource-shaped **run-target editor** (PR #246 — closes the flat-file + Unity Catalog target inputs, making suites runnable); **check version-history drawer** (#280; backend #281 + frontend #282 — view-only, restore deferred to #283); **suite export/import UI** (PR-E1); **suite sharing panel** (PR-E2 backend + PR-E3 UI, gated on `SuiteRead.my_permission`); **GX-Cloud redesign complete (4/4)** — classified connection + check create pages, edit-only drawers, suite-route/categorized picker (PR-A1/A2/B1/B2); PR-3c polish 3/3 (Settings `extra="forbid"` shipped via the `.env`/`.env.app` split, #209); **Monaco custom-SQL editor** (ADR 0019 — custom-SQL as a GX `UnexpectedRowsExpectation`; backend #258 + frontend #259); **admin control centre** (#289 — workspace-admin authz + `/admin/{suites,users,access}` + the `/admin` page & 403, pulling the Week-7 prototype-adoption admin tasks forward))_

## Week 5 — Execution engine + scheduling

**Exit gate:** Async runs with live progress across all datasource types; scheduling operational.

### Async execution backend (9 tasks — 4/9 ✅, early)
- [x] ✅ Celery + Redis background task runner for GX scan execution — `run_suite` task + `run_service` — landed early via [PR 4a](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/74) + [PR 4c-i](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/78) + [PR 4c-ii](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/79)
- [x] ✅ Generalise `run_suite` worker dispatch — select the `CheckRunner` by `connection.type` via the runner registry (`build_check_runner`), replacing the Snowflake-hardcoded wiring in `worker/tasks.py`; the seam that makes the flat-file / UC run paths route correctly and post-v1 RDBMS adapters a drop-in — [PR #146-fix](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/146) _(added per [ADR 0011](adr/0011-extensibility-seams-for-deferred-integrations.md); profiler dispatch collapsed onto a parallel registry in the same effort, [#147](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/147))_
- [x] ✅ **Check target table/path** — modeled as a **per-suite** datasource-shaped `Suite.target` (JSONB, shaped like the column-profiler request: `table`/`schema`/`catalog`/`path`/`file_format`), since `execute_run` runs all of a suite's checks against one batch (the GX data-asset-per-suite shape). `services/run_target.resolve_target` resolves it per connection type to the runner's `(table, schema, catalog)` triple (flat-file path rides the table-shaped `CheckRunner`'s `table` slot); `validate_target` is the write-time 422 guard on suite create/update. Migration `b1f2c3d4e5a6` (additive nullable column, tested up/down on Postgres). **Dispatch ungated**: `_trigger_suites` now hands each created run to `run_suite.delay` (lazy import — worker↔service cycle), broker-fail marks the run `failed`; `run_suite` resolves the target from the suite (targetless → clean `failed`), so the worker no longer takes a `table` arg; stale `until_week3` wording removed. 13 resolver units + worker/orchestration/suite-API target tests. _(Re-tracked from Week 3; [#215](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/215); PR-C0a)_
- [x] ✅ **Manual run trigger + run/result read API** — `POST /suites/{id}/run` queues a `Run` and dispatches it via the shared `run_dispatch.dispatch_run` helper (**edit**-gated — the capability ladder grants "trigger runs" at edit); the suite target (#215) is resolved **up front** so a targetless/misconfigured suite fails fast with 422 instead of a queued→failed run, and a broker outage marks the run `failed` + returns 503 (same contract as the probe). New `api/v1/runs.py` read surface for the results page: `GET /runs?suite_id=&status=&limit=` and `GET /runs/{id}` (run + joined `Result`s) are **suite-scoped** — the list filters to accessible suites and the detail gates on `require_permission(view)`, so per-suite sharing + existence-hiding hold (exactly why direct-Postgres/Grafana is rejected as the primary surface); `GET /pipeline_runs?provider=&status=` is the orchestration monitoring feed (auth-only, not suite-scoped). Read helpers live in `run_service.list_runs/get_run/list_results` + `orchestration_service.list_pipeline_runs`. 14 DB-backed API tests (trigger happy/targetless-422/edit-403/no-access-404/broker-503; list scoping+filters+limit; detail+results / 404s; pipeline filters + auth). _(Run-enablement gap; PR-C0b; gates the Week-6 Results page PR-C1)_
- [ ] ⬜ Run progress API — poll endpoint returning per-check live status
- [ ] ⬜ Cancel run endpoint — gracefully terminate in-progress Celery task
- [ ] ⬜ Run history retention policy — configurable purge of results older than N days
- [ ] ⬜ Flat file run path — resolve batch, load via Pandas, execute GX suite
- [ ] ⬜ UC run path — submit job to Databricks SQL Warehouse, execute GX suite

### ADF + Airflow hybrid monitoring (webhook + polling fallback) (4 tasks — 2/4 ✅)
- [x] ✅ Celery beat task — poll ADF REST API every 10 min for **succeeded** runs (skip pipelines updated by webhook recently) — provider-agnostic `poll_orchestration_runs` beat task through the `OrchestrationProvider` seam — [PR #171](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/189)
- [x] ✅ Celery beat task — poll Airflow REST API `dagRuns` every 10 min — same provider-agnostic beat task (ADF + Airflow share it) — [PR #171](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/189) _(added per ADR 0004; not in roadmap)_
- [ ] ⬜ Gap recovery logic — on startup + every 30 min, fetch last hour of run statuses
- [ ] ⬜ `GET /api/v1/orchestration/pipelines` — latest status per pipeline/DAG, provider-agnostic

> **`trigger_bindings` CRUD** (provider-agnostic suite-run triggers — `provider`/`pipeline_or_dag_id`/`env` → `suite_id`) landed early via [PR #172](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/190), unblocking the trigger-on-success path skeletoned in Week 2 PR 8.

### Execution UI (5 tasks — 1/5 ✅)
- [ ] ⬜ Run now panel — suite picker, env / datasource, notification target
- [ ] ⬜ Live run progress UI — check-by-check status with spinner + cancel button
- [ ] ⬜ Scheduled runs table — create, pause, delete cron schedules
- [ ] ⬜ Recent runs audit table with drill-down link to results
- [x] ✅ **Suite Triggers panel** — UI over the `trigger_bindings` CRUD (#172): bind/unbind a pipeline/DAG (`provider` + `pipeline_or_dag_id` + `env`) to a suite so it runs on the pipeline's success. **Suite-level**, not a check field (orchestration providers are never a datasource/check attribute, CLAUDE.md §4); inline `TriggersPanel` card on the suite detail — list + enable/disable toggle + remove + add form (ADF/Airflow · pipeline-or-DAG id · env), edit-gated (read-only for viewers). `api/triggerBindings.ts` + 6 component tests. _(PR #247; closes [#216](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/216))_

**Week 5 total: 7 / 18** _(early credit: Celery task runner in PR 4; worker runner-dispatch #146; ADF + Airflow 10-min polling #171; plus `trigger_bindings` CRUD #172 out-of-band; check target-table + dispatch ungate #215 (PR-C0a); manual run trigger + run/result read API (PR-C0b); **Suite Triggers UI #216 (PR #247)**. +3 tasks re-tracked here: check target-table #215 (slipped from W3), Suite Triggers UI #216, and the run-enablement read API (PR-C0b). The **manual Run button** on the suite detail (PR #245, the UI for the PR-C0b trigger endpoint) + the datasource-shaped **run-target editor** on the suite drawer (PR #246, makes UI-created suites runnable) also shipped this stretch)_

## Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting

**Exit gate:** Full results dashboard live across all source types; alerts firing with suppression.

### Results dashboard (10 tasks — 2/10 ✅)
> **Build as an in-app React page** (the GX-Cloud-style redesign Phase C), **not Grafana** — reading Postgres directly would bypass DataQ's per-suite sharing + PII redaction; Grafana is deferred to an optional post-v1 ops add-on ([ADR 0018](adr/0018-results-surface-and-grafana-deferral.md), accepted). The Week-5 run-enablement backend it gates on is **done**: `runs.py` read endpoints (`GET /runs`, `GET /runs/{id}`, `GET /pipeline_runs`) + `POST /suites/{id}/run` (PR-C0b); [#215](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/215) target (PR-C0a). The `results.metric_value`/`duration_ms` columns were seam-built for these trend charts (ADR 0012).
- [x] ✅ **Results page scaffold** (PR-C1) — the in-app `/results` page + sidebar nav (Connections · Suites · **Results** · Profile). **Runs tab**: a runs table (suite name, status, triggered-by, started, duration) with a status filter, each row opening a **run-detail drawer** that drills into the per-check results (check name + expectation, severity tag, `metric_value`, observed value). **Pipeline runs tab**: the orchestration monitoring feed (`pipeline_runs`) with a provider filter. Pure presentation helpers (`resultsFormat.ts` — duration/timestamp formatters, status→colour maps) are unit-tested; `api/runs.ts` mirrors the C0b schemas. Demo seed (`demo_data.py`) now lands runs + results (pass/pass/warn/fail spread) + two pipeline-runs + suite targets so the page has real content. 10 vitest + 2 Playwright (`results.spec.ts`). _The rich widgets below build on this scaffold._
- [ ] ⬜ Health score stat cards + 7-day trend chart
- [ ] ⬜ Per-suite pass / fail progress bars — warn / fail / critical breakdown
- [ ] 🟡 Results filter bar — env, datasource type, suite, date range, status — _run **status** filter + pipeline **provider** filter shipped (PR-C1); env/datasource/suite/date-range pending_
- [ ] 🟡 Failed check drill-down — sample failing rows from GX result — _per-check drill-down shipped (PR-C1); **sample rows withheld** pending row-level PII redaction ([#226](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/226), ADR 0018)_
- [ ] ⬜ Per-check historical trend chart
- [ ] 🟡 Orchestration status panel — pipeline/DAG status, polls every 30s, correlated DQ result — _pipeline-runs feed + status shipped (PR-C1, `/pipeline_runs` tab); 30s auto-poll + DQ-run correlation pending_
- [ ] ⬜ Datasource type filter — Snowflake / flat file / Unity Catalog toggle
- [ ] ⬜ CSV + PDF export of results
- [x] ✅ Severity badge colours — green / amber / red / dark red — severity-tier (pass/warn/fail/critical) + run-status tag colour maps in `resultsFormat.ts`, rendered as antd Tags on the runs table + run detail (PR-C1)
- [ ] ⬜ Health score weighting — apply warn/fail/critical penalty weights

### Alerting (6 tasks — 0/6)
- [ ] ⬜ `ResultPublisher` seam — dispatch run outcomes from the post-`execute_run` completion point through a small publisher interface (Teams is the v1 implementation, not a hardcoded call); carry a PII redaction / opt-in policy on `sample_failures` at the seam since it leaves DataQ's trust boundary. Enables post-v1 TestRail / JIRA / Xray publishers as additional subscribers with no re-plumbing _(added per [ADR 0011](adr/0011-extensibility-seams-for-deferred-integrations.md))_
- [ ] ⬜ Notification config UI — Teams webhook per suite, alert on fail / warn / always
- [ ] ⬜ Alert suppression / snooze — silence a specific check for N hours
- [ ] ⬜ Alert dedup — fire on first failure only, not on every subsequent scheduled run
- [ ] ⬜ Teams adaptive card payload — check, datasource, table / file, observed vs expected
- [ ] ⬜ Severity-aware alert routing — warn quiet, fail standard, critical @channel

### Prototype adoption — dedicated-page flows + Dashboard landing (added — not in original roadmap) (6 tasks — 0/6) — tracked in epic [#175](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/175)
> Adopt the **DataQ Design System** prototype (claude.ai/design project *"DataQ Design System"*, reverse-engineered from the shipped indigo/antd app — its `readme.md` is the spec). The **foundation already matches** the product (`theme.ts` palette, `resultsFormat.ts` status model, the 56px-header/220px-sider shell), so this is **not a re-skin** — it adopts the prototype's **structure**: every create/edit flow becomes a dedicated, deep-linkable **page** (the **Share** drawer stays the *only* drawer), login lands on an **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard**, and standard **error pages** ship. Components are reproduced with **real antd** — the prototype's framework-free recreations + `_ds_bundle.js` are reference only (per the design README). Backend-gated screens (Admin, Settings) + post-v1 items (marketing, dark mode) are tracked in Week 7 + Post-v1 below. The intentionally-dropped prototype bits (the **"View as" role switch**, the **component bundle**, the **teal palette**) are recorded in Post-v1 as *not adopted*.
>
> **Design source — how to open the prototype (read before picking up any task in this track):** the claude.ai/design project **"DataQ Design System"** — `projectId` **`317fec67-2b8a-498c-8f6b-523750916a8d`** (owner Arijit). Read it with the **`/design-sync`** skill / **`DesignSync`** tool over the claude.ai login: `DesignSync method=list_files projectId=…`, then `method=get_file path=<…>`. Key paths: **`readme.md`** = the canonical spec (voice · color · type · spacing · status model · component/template manifest); **`guidelines/*.card.html`** = foundation specimen cards; **`templates/app/*.jsx`** = framework-free recreations of each screen; **`components/<group>/*.jsx`** = the component recreations; **`tokens/*.css`** = raw token values; **`uploads/DESIGN.md`** = the *superseded teal* brief (ignore for palette). ⚠️ The hosted share link `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/design/h/SVkQJqee7xsc2FNWVOsnIQ` returns **404** — reach the prototype via DesignSync, not the link. The `.jsx` / `_ds_bundle.js` are **reference only** — build in real antd reading `theme.ts`.

Change the login redirect `/` → `/dashboard` (was `/connections`). Touches `App.tsx` `NAV_ITEMS` + `<Routes>` only — the nav already prefix-matches ([#192](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/192)). _Design ref: `templates/app/AppShell.jsx` (white header + light sider + footer nav) + readme §"What's in here → Templates → `templates/app`" (the sidebar + footer item lists)._

This is the **page home** for the "Health score stat cards", "Per-suite pass/fail bars" and "Per-check historical trend chart" tasks above — build them here rather than on `/results`. **Prereq:** a chart-library decision (antd ships no charts → `@ant-design/charts` or `recharts`; runs through `pnpm audit` + bundle-size gate → ADR-light note). Render only backend-backed KPIs (see the W7 *KPI honesty pass*). _Design ref: `templates/app/DashboardScreen.jsx`; components `data/MetricCard.jsx`, `data/Card.jsx`, `feedback/ProgressBar.jsx`, `data/DataTable.jsx`; readme §"Templates" (Enhanced Monitoring — KPIs · Quality Trends · Suite Performance · Recent Runs)._
- [ ] ⬜ **New Suite → dedicated page** (`/suites/new`, `SuiteFlow`) — extract the `SuiteDrawer` **create** path into a routed *details / source* page reusing `suiteTarget.ts` + the run-target editor (PR #246). Removes the drawer's create branch; `Suites.tsx` swaps `setDrawer({open:true})` for `navigate('/suites/new')`. _Design ref: `templates/app/SuiteFlow.jsx` (New Suite → Details/Source) → `CheckFlow.jsx` (Define Rules); readme §"Templates" full-page-flows note._
- [ ] ⬜ **Edit flows → dedicated pages** — unify edit onto the existing create pages via an `:id` route + prefill, reusing the already-extracted form specs (`connectionFormSpec.ts` / `checkForm.ts` / `suiteTarget.ts`): connection edit `/connections/:id/edit` → `ConnectionNew`; suite edit `/suites/:id/edit` → `SuiteFlow`; check edit `/suites/:id/checks/:checkId/edit` → `CheckNew`. **Removes** the `ConnectionDrawer` / `CheckDrawer` / `SuiteDrawer` **edit** drawers; the parent pages drop their `editing` / `drawer` state for `navigate()`. (Dedups with [#204](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/204).) _Design ref: `templates/app/ConnectionFlow.jsx` (Configuration), `SuiteFlow.jsx`, `CheckFlow.jsx` — the create flows; edit reuses the same page shape, prefilled._
- [ ] ⬜ **Results drill-down → dedicated route** (`/results/:runId`, `ResultsScreen` detail) — promote the in-page `openRun` `useState` ([Results.tsx:54](../frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx#L54)) to a routed run-detail page with back-nav, per-check results (severity tags + `metric_value` + observed), and CSV/JSON download. Deep-linkable + refreshable (the prototype's `ResultsScreen` flow). _Design ref: `templates/app/ResultsScreen.jsx`; readme §"Templates" (Results → click a run → Run detail with per-check results + CSV/JSON download)._
- [ ] ⬜ **Standard error pages (4xx / 5xx)** — an `ErrorState`-based page in the app shell switchable across 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 429 / 500 / 502 / 503 / 504; wire it into the router catch-all + the `ErrorBoundary` ([PR #210](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/210)) fallback so render-time + routing failures render in-brand. _Design ref: `templates/error/index.html` + `components/feedback/ErrorState.jsx` (+ `components/feedback/errorstate.card.html`); readme §"Templates → `templates/error/`"._

**Week 6 total: 2 / 22** _(early credit: Results page scaffold + severity badge colours (PR-C1, the GX-Cloud redesign Phase C); 3 dashboard tasks partially landed — status/provider filters, per-check drill-down, pipeline-runs feed. +6 prototype-adoption tasks added: nav/shell, Dashboard landing page, suite-create page, edit-flows→pages, results drill-down route, error pages.)_

## Week 7 — Deployment, hardening & docs

**Exit gate:** Production-ready v1 deployed to Azure, CI/CD live, team onboarded.

### DevOps & deployment (6 tasks — 0/6, 1 partial early)
- [ ] 🟡 Containerise FastAPI + React + Celery + Redis — backend `Dockerfile` + `api`/`worker` compose services landed early ([PR 4a](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/74)); React image + ACR/ACA still pending

App Insights stays the only v1 backend; do not abstract speculatively)_

content.text:
Here's where **Week 5 — Execution engine + scheduling** stands. Exit gate: *async runs with live progress across all datasource types; scheduling operational.*

## Already done — 7/18 (mostly early credit)

**Async execution backend (4/9)**
- ✅ Celery + Redis task runner (`run_suite` + `run_service`) — PR 4
- ✅ Worker dispatch generalised — `CheckRunner` picked by `connection.type` via registry — #146
- ✅ Check target table/path — per-suite `Suite.target` + `run_target` resolver, dispatch ungated — #215 (PR-C0a)
- ✅ Manual run trigger + run/result read API — `POST /suites/{id}/run` + `GET /runs`·`/runs/{id}`·`/pipeline_runs` — PR-C0b

**ADF + Airflow hybrid monitoring (2/4)**
- ✅ 10-min polling beat for ADF — provider-agnostic `poll_orchestration_runs` — #171
- ✅ 10-min polling beat for Airflow (same task) — #171
- (plus `trigger_bindings` CRUD landed out-of-band — #172)

**Execution UI (1/5)**
- ✅ Suite Triggers panel — UI over `trigger_bindings` — #216 (PR #247)

## Waiting for us — 11 tasks

**Async execution backend (5 left)**
- ⬜ Run progress API — poll endpoint returning per-check live status
- ⬜ Cancel run endpoint — gracefully terminate in-progress Celery task
- ⬜ Run history retention policy — configurable purge of results older than N days
- ⬜ Flat-file run path — resolve batch, load via Pandas, execute GX suite
- ⬜ UC run path — submit to Databricks SQL Warehouse, execute GX suite

**ADF + Airflow hybrid monitoring (2 left)**
- ⬜ Gap recovery — on startup + every 30 min, fetch last hour of run statuses, upsert missed events
- ⬜ `GET /api/v1/orchestration/pipelines` — latest status per pipeline/DAG (provider-agnostic, DB-sourced)

**Execution UI (4 left)**
- ⬜ Run now panel — suite picker, env/datasource, notification target
- ⬜ Live run progress UI — check-by-check status + spinner + cancel button
- ⬜ Scheduled runs table — create, pause, delete cron schedules
- ⬜ Recent runs audit table with drill-down to results

## Notes on the critical path

- **Scheduling** (the second half of the exit gate) has no explicit *backend* task in the list, but the "Scheduled runs table" UI implies a cron/Celery-beat scheduler for suite runs — that backend likely needs to land before (or alongside) the UI. Worth flagging as a sub-task.
- The **flat-file and UC run paths** are the last pieces of "runs across all datasource types." The runners (`FlatFileCheckRunner`, `UnityCatalogCheckRunner`) and batch resolution already exist from Week 3 behind the shared `gx_runner` — these tasks are about wiring the live download/read seams into `run_suite`, not building runners from scratch.
- **Progress API + cancel** pair together and feed the "Live run progress UI."

Want me to draft a sequencing plan for the remaining 11, or just pick the first task and start? My recommendation for ordering: **(1)** flat-file + UC run paths (completes the "all datasource types" gate), **(2)** run progress + cancel API, **(3)** scheduling backend + UI, **(4)** orchestration `/pipelines` + gap recovery, **(5)** remaining execution UI.

content.text: Let me look at the Week-5-milestoned issues in detail — those are the in-scope prerequisites.

content.content:
===== #174 =====
TITLE: Week 5 — Execution engine + scheduling (tracking epic)
LABELS: epic

Tracking epic for **Week 5 — Execution engine + scheduling**. Mirrors the roadmap; the task-level source of truth remains [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md). Milestone: `Week 5 — Execution engine + scheduling`.

**Progress (early-credit):** 4 done / 17.

**Async execution backend**
- [x] Celery + Redis background task runner for GX scan execution — landed early (PR 4a/4c)
- [x] Generalise `run_suite` worker dispatch — select `CheckRunner` by `connection.type` via the runner registry (`build_check_runner`) — #146 _(ADR 0011; profiler dispatch collapsed onto a parallel registry, #147)_
- [ ] **Check target table/path** — checks carry no target today (dry-run takes `table` in the body), so the trigger-on-success path is gated (`suite_dispatch_deferred`). The prerequisite for every run path; model the target, migrate, ungate dispatch. Slipped from Week 3 → tracked here — #215
- [ ] Run progress API — poll endpoint returning per-check live status
- [ ] Cancel run endpoint — gracefully terminate in-progress Celery task
- [ ] Run history retention policy — configurable purge of results older than N days
- [ ] Flat file run path — resolve batch, load via Pandas, execute GX suite
- [ ] UC run path — submit job to Databricks SQL Warehouse, execute GX suite

**ADF + Airflow hybrid monitoring (webhook + polling fallback)**
- [x] Celery beat task — poll ADF REST API every 10 min for **succeeded** runs, skip recently-webhooked pipelines — provider-agnostic `poll_orchestration_runs` — #171
- [x] Celery beat task — poll Airflow REST API `dagRuns` every 10 min — same provider-agnostic beat task — #171
- [ ] Gap recovery logic — on startup + every 30 min, fetch last hour of run statuses and upsert missed events
- [ ] `GET /api/v1/orchestration/pipelines` — latest status per pipeline/DAG, provider-agnostic

**Execution UI**
- [ ] Run now panel — suite picker, env / datasource, notification target
- [ ] Live run progress UI — check-by-check status with spinner + cancel button
- [ ] Scheduled runs table — create, pause, delete cron schedules
- [ ] Recent runs audit table with drill-down link to results
- [ ] **Suite Triggers panel** — UI over the shipped `trigger_bindings` CRUD (#172): suite-level bind of pipeline/DAG → suite so it runs on the pipeline's success (not a check field, CLAUDE.md §4) — #216

_Out-of-band: `trigger_bindings` CRUD (provider-agnostic suite-run triggers) landed via #172._

===== #212 =====
TITLE: create_suite accepts an orchestration connection (ADF/Airflow) as a suite target
LABELS: bug, priority/P2

Surfaced by the full-stack E2E smoke (#211 review).

## Problem
`suite_service.create_suite` validates only that `connection_id` references an existing connection — **not** that the connection is a *datasource*. So `POST /suites` with an ADF or Airflow `connection_id` returns **201**, binding a suite to an orchestration provider.

This violates the core boundary (CLAUDE.md §4 / §11): ADF + Airflow are orchestration providers, **not** queryable datasources — you can't author/run DQ checks against them. A suite bound to one is a semantically invalid, unrunnable state.

(The E2E smoke originally tripped over this — `GET /connections` is `created_at DESC`, so the last-seeded Airflow connection was `conns[0]`, and the round-trip happily created a suite on it. The smoke was fixed to pick a datasource; the backend gap remains.)

## Fix
In `create_suite` (and the suite import path), reject a `connection.type` in the orchestration set (`adf`, `airflow`) with a 422 (e.g. `suite_connection_not_datasource`). Reuse the datasource/orchestration split already encoded in the registries rather than hardcoding the two names.

## Acceptance
- [ ] `POST /suites` with an ADF/Airflow connection → 422 (not 201)
- [ ] Suite import with an orchestration connection → 422
- [ ] A datasource connection (snowflake/s3/adls_gen2/unity_catalog) still works
- [ ] Test covering the rejection

===== #122 =====
TITLE: Add operational result statuses: skip + error (additive CHECK widening)
LABELS: enhancement, priority/P1

## Context
The Week-3 schema seam ([#121](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/121)) retargeted `results.status` to ADR 0005's **severity tiers** — `pass / warn / fail / critical` — and deliberately **dropped the previously-unused `skipped`** value (it was never emitted by the run path).

That decision was explicitly made on the basis that adding operational states back later is a **cheap, additive `CHECK` widening** — the opposite of the expensive tier-*narrowing* ADR 0005 front-loaded. This issue tracks doing exactly that when the run path needs them.

## Ask
Add two **operational** (non-severity) result statuses, orthogonal to the four health-score tiers:

- **`skip`** — the check did not evaluate (e.g. not applicable to this batch, gated off, batch/file missing).
- **`error`** — the check threw / could not be evaluated (distinct from `fail`, which is a *successful evaluation* that breached an expectation). Today a per-check exception drives the whole **run** to `failed` (`run_service`); a per-result `error` lets a single check error without failing siblings.

## Implementation notes
- Additive migration: widen the `ck_results_status_valid` CHECK to `('pass','warn','fail','critical','skip','error')`. No data rewrite, no two-step (pure widening).
- Add to `RESULT_STATUSES` in `db/models.py`.
- **Health-score SQL must exclude `skip`/`error` from `N`** — they carry no penalty weight (ADR 0005 only defines weights for the four tiers). i.e. aggregate `WHERE status IN ('pass','warn','fail','critical')`.
- `run_service` (and the future `check.kind` dispatch / severity post-processing) emit them where appropriate.

## When
Pick up alongside the run-path work that first produces them — severity post-processing / `check.kind` dispatch (Week 3) or the execution-engine hardening (Week 5). Not blocking; the seam was left intentionally cheap to extend.

Refs: ADR 0005 (severity tiers), ADR 0012 (`check.kind`), PR #121 (the drop decision).

===== #227 =====
TITLE: Dedupe the create-queued-run + dispatch + broker-failure pattern across trigger paths
LABELS: priority/P3, refactor

## Context
Three sites create a queued `Run`, hand it to `run_dispatch.dispatch_run`, and on broker failure mark it terminal-`failed`:
- `api/v1/probe.py` `trigger_snowflake_probe`
- `api/v1/suites.py` `trigger_suite_run` (manual run, PR-C0b #225)
- `services/orchestration_service.py` `_trigger_suites` (pipeline-success, batch)

The broker-failure DB shape had **already diverged** once: `_trigger_suites` set `status=failed` **and** `finished_at=now()`, while `probe` and the new manual path set only `status`. PR-C0b aligned the manual path (finished_at set), but `probe.py` still leaves `finished_at` NULL, and the create+dispatch+503 block is duplicated.

## Ask
Extract a shared helper (e.g. `run_dispatch.create_and_dispatch_run(session, *, suite_id, triggered_by) -> Run`, or a `mark_dispatch_failed(run)` for the failure shape) so the terminal-failed contract is one decision. Note: `_trigger_suites` dispatches a **batch** in a loop, so the helper must fit that shape or split into create vs failure-shape pieces.

## Acceptance
- One definition of the dispatch-failure run shape; `probe.py` aligned (finished_at set).
- No behaviour change to happy paths; existing tests stay green.

_Surfaced by the PR-C0b multi-agent review (altitude/reuse)._

===== #147 =====
TITLE: Profiler / flat-file cleanup: timeout drift, duplicated format+stat logic, N+1 profiler queries
LABELS: priority/P1, refactor

Low-priority cleanup items surfaced during the full `/code-review` of `58193cd..HEAD` (column profiler + check runners, #137–#144). Grouped here; none is a correctness bug. The correctness/robustness findings are tracked separately in #145, and the dispatch/registry gap in #146.

### 1. S3 read-timeout drift (30s → 60s)
When the flat-file download was extracted into `flatfile.py`, the S3 read timeout changed from the profiler's original 30s (`profile_service._NETWORK_TIMEOUT`) to [`_READ_TIMEOUT = 60`](backend/app/datasources/flatfile.py#L38). More lenient than before; a hung S3 read now ties up a worker thread twice as long. Confirm 60s is intentional (full-file reads may legitimately need longer than a profiler sample) vs accidental drift, and align the two constants.

### 2. `_SUPPORTED_FORMATS` duplicates `format_from_path`
[`infer_file_format`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L333) calls `flatfile.format_from_path(path)` then checks membership in [`_SUPPORTED_FORMATS = {"csv","parquet"}`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L62). But `format_from_path` already only ever returns `csv`/`parquet`/`None`, so the supported-format set is expressed twice. Add JSON to `format_from_path` and the profiler silently still rejects it. Derive the supported set from `format_from_path`, or drop the redundant check.

### 3. Duplicated stat contract: SQL vs pandas
The per-column statistic definitions (null_fraction = nulls/row_count with the row_count==0 guard, distinct excludes nulls, top_values = non-null desc by count) are implemented twice with no shared code — once in SQL via [`assemble_profile`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L195) and once in pandas via [`profile_dataframe`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L362). A definitional change must be made in both or the two datasource families disagree for the same logical data. (Hard to fully DRY across SQL vs pandas, but the *contract* could be captured in one place / a shared test.)

### 4. N+1 warehouse round-trips in the SQL profiler
[`profile_table`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L304) issues 1 aggregate query + one top-values query **per column** in a Python loop. For a 50-column profile that's 51 sequential warehouse queries; against a cold SQL warehouse (~1s/query) that's ~50s of wall time for an interactive authoring aid. Acceptable for v1 (≤50-col cap), but consider batching the top-values queries (UNION ALL / a single windowed query) if profiler latency becomes a problem.

### 5. Parquet footer read twice (minor)
[`_read_dataframe`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L440) reads `pq.ParquetFile(raw).schema.names` to filter requested columns to those present, then re-parses with `pd.read_parquet(columns=present)`. The footer read is cheap, and it's needed so `read_parquet` doesn't raise on an absent column — but the column-presence logic now lives in two places (the pre-read filter and `profile_dataframe`'s missing-column 422 check). Worth a glance to ensure they can't diverge.

Pick up opportunistically; suggest a single cleanup PR (`Fixes #<this>`).

- [x] Celery beat task — poll Airflow REST API `dagRuns` every 10 min — same provider-agnostic beat task — #171
- [ ] Gap recovery logic — on startup + every 30 min, fetch last hour of run statuses and upsert missed events
- [ ] `GET /api/v1/orchestration/pipelines` — latest status per pipeline/DAG, provider-agnostic

**Execution UI**
- [ ] Run now panel — suite picker, env / datasource, notification target
- [ ] Live run progress UI — check-by-check status with spinner + cancel button
- [ ] Scheduled runs table — create, pause, delete cron schedules
- [ ] Recent runs audit table with drill-down link to results
- [ ] **Suite Triggers panel** — UI over the shipped `trigger_bindings` CRUD (#172): suite-level bind of pipeline/DAG → suite so it runs on the pipeline's success (not a check field, CLAUDE.md §4) — #216

_Out-of-band: `trigger_bindings` CRUD (provider-agnostic suite-run triggers) landed via #172._

===== #212 =====
TITLE: create_suite accepts an orchestration connection (ADF/Airflow) as a suite target
LABELS: bug, priority/P2

Surfaced by the full-stack E2E smoke (#211 review).

## Problem
`suite_service.create_suite` validates only that `connection_id` references an existing connection — **not** that the connection is a *datasource*. So `POST /suites` with an ADF or Airflow `connection_id` returns **201**, binding a suite to an orchestration provider.

This violates the core boundary (CLAUDE.md §4 / §11): ADF + Airflow are orchestration providers, **not** queryable datasources — you can't author/run DQ checks against them. A suite bound to one is a semantically invalid, unrunnable state.

(The E2E smoke originally tripped over this — `GET /connections` is `created_at DESC`, so the last-seeded Airflow connection was `conns[0]`, and the round-trip happily created a suite on it. The smoke was fixed to pick a datasource; the backend gap remains.)

## Fix
In `create_suite` (and the suite import path), reject a `connection.type` in the orchestration set (`adf`, `airflow`) with a 422 (e.g. `suite_connection_not_datasource`). Reuse the datasource/orchestration split already encoded in the registries rather than hardcoding the two names.

## Acceptance
- [ ] `POST /suites` with an ADF/Airflow connection → 422 (not 201)
- [ ] Suite import with an orchestration connection → 422
- [ ] A datasource connection (snowflake/s3/adls_gen2/unity_catalog) still works
- [ ] Test covering the rejection

===== #122 =====
TITLE: Add operational result statuses: skip + error (additive CHECK widening)
LABELS: enhancement, priority/P1

## Context
The Week-3 schema seam ([#121](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/121)) retargeted `results.status` to ADR 0005's **severity tiers** — `pass / warn / fail / critical` — and deliberately **dropped the previously-unused `skipped`** value (it was never emitted by the run path).

That decision was explicitly made on the basis that adding operational states back later is a **cheap, additive `CHECK` widening** — the opposite of the expensive tier-*narrowing* ADR 0005 front-loaded. This issue tracks doing exactly that when the run path needs them.

## Ask
Add two **operational** (non-severity) result statuses, orthogonal to the four health-score tiers:

- **`skip`** — the check did not evaluate (e.g. not applicable to this batch, gated off, batch/file missing).
- **`error`** — the check threw / could not be evaluated (distinct from `fail`, which is a *successful evaluation* that breached an expectation). Today a per-check exception drives the whole **run** to `failed` (`run_service`); a per-result `error` lets a single check error without failing siblings.

## Implementation notes
- Additive migration: widen the `ck_results_status_valid` CHECK to `('pass','warn','fail','critical','skip','error')`. No data rewrite, no two-step (pure widening).
- Add to `RESULT_STATUSES` in `db/models.py`.
- **Health-score SQL must exclude `skip`/`error` from `N`** — they carry no penalty weight (ADR 0005 only defines weights for the four tiers). i.e. aggregate `WHERE status IN ('pass','warn','fail','critical')`.
- `run_service` (and the future `check.kind` dispatch / severity post-processing) emit them where appropriate.

## When
Pick up alongside the run-path work that first produces them — severity post-processing / `check.kind` dispatch (Week 3) or the execution-engine hardening (Week 5). Not blocking; the seam was left intentionally cheap to extend.

Refs: ADR 0005 (severity tiers), ADR 0012 (`check.kind`), PR #121 (the drop decision).

===== #227 =====
TITLE: Dedupe the create-queued-run + dispatch + broker-failure pattern across trigger paths
LABELS: priority/P3, refactor

## Context
Three sites create a queued `Run`, hand it to `run_dispatch.dispatch_run`, and on broker failure mark it terminal-`failed`:
- `api/v1/probe.py` `trigger_snowflake_probe`
- `api/v1/suites.py` `trigger_suite_run` (manual run, PR-C0b #225)
- `services/orchestration_service.py` `_trigger_suites` (pipeline-success, batch)

The broker-failure DB shape had **already diverged** once: `_trigger_suites` set `status=failed` **and** `finished_at=now()`, while `probe` and the new manual path set only `status`. PR-C0b aligned the manual path (finished_at set), but `probe.py` still leaves `finished_at` NULL, and the create+dispatch+503 block is duplicated.

## Ask
Extract a shared helper (e.g. `run_dispatch.create_and_dispatch_run(session, *, suite_id, triggered_by) -> Run`, or a `mark_dispatch_failed(run)` for the failure shape) so the terminal-failed contract is one decision. Note: `_trigger_suites` dispatches a **batch** in a loop, so the helper must fit that shape or split into create vs failure-shape pieces.

## Acceptance
- One definition of the dispatch-failure run shape; `probe.py` aligned (finished_at set).
- No behaviour change to happy paths; existing tests stay green.

_Surfaced by the PR-C0b multi-agent review (altitude/reuse)._

===== #147 =====
TITLE: Profiler / flat-file cleanup: timeout drift, duplicated format+stat logic, N+1 profiler queries
LABELS: priority/P1, refactor

Low-priority cleanup items surfaced during the full `/code-review` of `58193cd..HEAD` (column profiler + check runners, #137–#144). Grouped here; none is a correctness bug. The correctness/robustness findings are tracked separately in #145, and the dispatch/registry gap in #146.

### 1. S3 read-timeout drift (30s → 60s)
When the flat-file download was extracted into `flatfile.py`, the S3 read timeout changed from the profiler's original 30s (`profile_service._NETWORK_TIMEOUT`) to [`_READ_TIMEOUT = 60`](backend/app/datasources/flatfile.py#L38). More lenient than before; a hung S3 read now ties up a worker thread twice as long. Confirm 60s is intentional (full-file reads may legitimately need longer than a profiler sample) vs accidental drift, and align the two constants.

### 2. `_SUPPORTED_FORMATS` duplicates `format_from_path`
[`infer_file_format`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L333) calls `flatfile.format_from_path(path)` then checks membership in [`_SUPPORTED_FORMATS = {"csv","parquet"}`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L62). But `format_from_path` already only ever returns `csv`/`parquet`/`None`, so the supported-format set is expressed twice. Add JSON to `format_from_path` and the profiler silently still rejects it. Derive the supported set from `format_from_path`, or drop the redundant check.

### 3. Duplicated stat contract: SQL vs pandas
The per-column statistic definitions (null_fraction = nulls/row_count with the row_count==0 guard, distinct excludes nulls, top_values = non-null desc by count) are implemented twice with no shared code — once in SQL via [`assemble_profile`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L195) and once in pandas via [`profile_dataframe`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L362). A definitional change must be made in both or the two datasource families disagree for the same logical data. (Hard to fully DRY across SQL vs pandas, but the *contract* could be captured in one place / a shared test.)

### 4. N+1 warehouse round-trips in the SQL profiler
[`profile_table`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L304) issues 1 aggregate query + one top-values query **per column** in a Python loop. For a 50-column profile that's 51 sequential warehouse queries; against a cold SQL warehouse (~1s/query) that's ~50s of wall time for an interactive authoring aid. Acceptable for v1 (≤50-col cap), but consider batching the top-values queries (UNION ALL / a single windowed query) if profiler latency becomes a problem.

### 5. Parquet footer read twice (minor)
[`_read_dataframe`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L440) reads `pq.ParquetFile(raw).schema.names` to filter requested columns to those present, then re-parses with `pd.read_parquet(columns=present)`. The footer read is cheap, and it's needed so `read_parquet` doesn't raise on an absent column — but the column-presence logic now lives in two places (the pre-read filter and `profile_dataframe`'s missing-column 422 check). Worth a glance to ensure they can't diverge.

Pick up opportunistically; suggest a single cleanup PR (`Fixes #<this>`).

lastPrompt: Also, can you check if there are any pre-requisite we need to complete before we start week 5 (like any open issues)

content.text:
Checked all open issues. **No hard blockers** — nothing prevents starting Week 5. But there are **5 issues milestoned to Week 5** (the tracking epic + 4 work items), and the honest read is that one is a genuine "do-first" and the rest are "do-alongside," not upfront.

## Week 5 issues

| # | Type | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| **#174** | epic | Just the tracking epic — not a task |
| **#212** | 🐛 bug (P2) | **Do first.** `create_suite` accepts an ADF/Airflow connection as a suite target → 201 instead of 422. Violates the core datasource/orchestration boundary (CLAUDE.md §4/§11) and produces an unrunnable suite. Cheap fix, and it's a genuine prerequisite to clean run-path semantics — you don't want the execution engine ever pointed at an orchestration connection. |
| **#122** | ⚡ enhancement (P1) | **Do alongside** the run paths. Adds `skip`/`error` result statuses (additive CHECK widening). The issue explicitly says *"not blocking… pick up alongside the run-path work that first produces them."* The flat-file run path (missing batch → `skip`) and per-check exceptions (→ `error`) are exactly where these get emitted. |
| **#147** | 🧹 refactor (P1) | **Do alongside** the flat-file/UC run path. Profiler/flat-file cleanup — timeout drift (30s vs 60s), duplicated CSV/Parquet stat logic, N+1 profiler queries. Touches the same `flatfile.py`/`profile_service.py` you'll be in for the flat-file run path. Not a blocker. |
| **#227** | 🧹 refactor (P3) | **Do alongside** the progress/cancel work. Dedupe the create-queued-run + dispatch + broker-failure pattern across the 3 trigger sites (`probe.py`, `suites.py`, `_trigger_suites`). Low priority; `probe.py` still leaves `finished_at` NULL on broker failure. Best folded into whichever PR next touches dispatch. |

## Bottom line

**Prerequisite to clear before run-path work: #212** (the P2 boundary bug) — small, self-contained, and it's the one thing that's actually *wrong* today rather than just unfinished. The other three are best merged into the relevant Week-5 PRs rather than done as standalone upfront work.

Everything else open is **Week 6/7/8 or post-v1 backlog** (none Week-5-relevant) — e.g. the Week-1 carryover to W7 (#168 token refresh, #169 real Key Vault, #170 prod-docs gate) stays parked for Week 7.

Want me to start with **#212** as the first PR, then move into the flat-file + UC run paths (picking up #122 and #147 there)? Or pull in a clean-branch check first?

1	"""Celery tasks for asynchronous suite execution.
2	
3	``run_suite`` is the worker entry point dispatched by the manual-run / probe /
4	pipeline-trigger paths. It loads the run's suite / connection / checks, resolves
5	the suite's datasource-shaped **target** (#215) to the runner's
6	``(table, schema, catalog)``, builds the datasource adapter, and hands off to
7	``run_service.execute_run``. The DB-touching core is factored into ``_run_suite``
8	so it can be unit-tested with a fake session + fake runner (no Postgres, no
9	Snowflake); real-DB integration coverage is a Week 8 item.
10	
11	The target lives on the suite (``Suite.target``, resolved by
12	``run_target.resolve_target``): a targetless suite drives the run to ``failed``
13	with a clear log rather than running against an unknown table.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import uuid
19	from datetime import UTC, datetime, timedelta
20	
21	from sqlalchemy import select
22	from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
23	
24	from backend.app.core.logging import get_logger
25	from backend.app.core.secrets import SecretStore, get_secret_store
26	from backend.app.datasources.registry import build_check_runner
27	from backend.app.db.models import ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS, Check, Connection, Run, Suite
28	from backend.app.db.session import get_session
29	from backend.app.orchestration.registry import get_orchestration_provider
30	from backend.app.services import orchestration_service, run_service, run_target
31	from backend.app.worker.celery_app import celery_app
32	
33	# Polling fallback (#171): look back slightly further than the 10-min beat
34	# interval so a run can't slip through the gap between consecutive polls.
35	_POLL_LOOKBACK = timedelta(minutes=15)
36	
37	log = get_logger(__name__)
38	
39	
40	def _run_suite(session: Session, *, run_id: uuid.UUID) -> str:
41	    """Load the run's graph, resolve its target, build the runner, execute.
42	
43	    The suite's datasource-shaped ``target`` (#215) resolves to the runner's
44	    ``(table, schema, catalog)`` via ``run_target.resolve_target``; dispatch by
45	    ``connection.type`` through the runner registry gives a Snowflake / Unity
46	    Catalog / flat-file suite its correct `CheckRunner` (#146).
47	
48	    Failures while loading, resolving the target (targetless or malformed suite),
49	    or building the runner (missing rows, bad connection config, unresolved
50	    secret) drive the run to ``failed`` so it never lingers in ``queued``;
51	    execution failures are handled inside ``execute_run``.
52	    """
53	    run = session.get(Run, run_id)
54	    if run is None:
55	        log.error("run_suite_run_not_found", run_id=str(run_id))
56	        return "not_found"
57	
58	    try:
59	        suite = session.get(Suite, run.suite_id)
60	        connection = session.get(Connection, suite.connection_id) if suite is not None else None
61	        if suite is None or connection is None:
62	            raise RuntimeError("suite or connection not found for run")
63	        target = run_target.resolve_target(connection.type, suite.target)
64	        checks = list(session.scalars(select(Check).where(Check.suite_id == suite.id)))
65	        runner = build_check_runner(
66	            conn_type=connection.type,
67	            config=connection.config,
68	            secret_ref=connection.secret_ref,
69	            secret_store=get_secret_store(),
70	            catalog=target.catalog,
71	        )
72	    except Exception:
73	        run.status = "failed"
74	        run.started_at = run.started_at or datetime.now(UTC)
75	        run.finished_at = datetime.now(UTC)
76	        session.commit()
77	        log.exception("run_suite_setup_failed", run_id=str(run_id))
78	        return "failed"
79	
80	    run_service.execute_run(
81	        session, run=run, checks=checks, runner=runner, table=target.table, schema=target.schema
82	    )
83	    return str(run.status)
84	
85	
86	@celery_app.task(name="run_suite")  # type: ignore[untyped-decorator]  # celery task decorator is unannotated
87	def run_suite(run_id: str) -> str:
88	    """Worker entry point. ``run_id`` is a string so it serialises over JSON.
89	
90	    The target is resolved from the suite (``Suite.target``), so the only
91	    argument the dispatcher supplies is the run id.
92	    """
93	    session = get_session()
94	    try:
95	        return _run_suite(session, run_id=uuid.UUID(run_id))
96	    finally:
97	        session.close()
98	
99	
100	def _poll_orchestration_runs(
101	    session: Session, *, secret_store: SecretStore, now: datetime | None = None
102	) -> dict[str, int]:
103	    """Poll every orchestrator connection for recent succeeded runs (#171, ADR 0004).
104	
105	    The polling fallback for runs that never produced a webhook: iterate each
106	    ADF / Airflow connection, ask the provider's `list_recent_runs` for runs
107	    updated within the lookback window, and hand them to `ingest_polled_runs`
108	    (upsert + trigger-on-success). Goes through the `OrchestrationProvider` seam —
109	    no per-provider branching. Each connection is isolated: a transport/auth
110	    failure logs + continues so one bad connection can't starve the rest.
111	    """
112	    since = (now or datetime.now(UTC)) - _POLL_LOOKBACK
113	    summary = {"connections": 0, "recorded": 0, "triggered": 0, "skipped": 0, "errors": 0}
114	    connections = list(
115	        session.scalars(
116	            select(Connection).where(
117	                Connection.type.in_(ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS),
118	                Connection.secret_ref.isnot(None),
119	            )
120	        )
121	    )
122	    for connection in connections:
123	        if not connection.secret_ref:  # defensive; the query already filters
124	            continue
125	        try:
126	            provider_impl = get_orchestration_provider(connection.type)
127	            secret = secret_store.get(connection.secret_ref)
128	            updates = provider_impl.list_recent_runs(dict(connection.config), secret, since)
129	            result = orchestration_service.ingest_polled_runs(
130	                session,
131	                provider_impl=provider_impl,
132	                connection=connection,
133	                updates=updates,
134	                skip_updated_since=since,
135	            )
136	            summary["connections"] += 1
137	            summary["recorded"] += len(result.pipeline_runs)
138	            summary["triggered"] += len(result.triggered_runs)
139	            summary["skipped"] += result.skipped
140	        except Exception:
141	            summary["errors"] += 1
142	            session.rollback()
143	            log.exception(
144	                "orchestration_poll_failed",
145	                connection_id=str(connection.id),
146	                provider=connection.type,
147	            )
148	    log.info("orchestration_poll_completed", **summary)
149	    return summary
150	
151	
152	@celery_app.task(name="poll_orchestration_runs")  # type: ignore[untyped-decorator]  # celery task decorator is unannotated
153	def poll_orchestration_runs() -> dict[str, int]:
154	    """Celery-beat entry point — the 10-min orchestration polling fallback."""
155	    session = get_session()
156	    try:
157	        return _poll_orchestration_runs(session, secret_store=get_secret_store())
158	    finally:
159	        session.close()

file.content:
"""Celery tasks for asynchronous suite execution.

``run_suite`` is the worker entry point dispatched by the manual-run / probe /
pipeline-trigger paths. It loads the run's suite / connection / checks, resolves
the suite's datasource-shaped **target** (#215) to the runner's
``(table, schema, catalog)``, builds the datasource adapter, and hands off to
``run_service.execute_run``. The DB-touching core is factored into ``_run_suite``
so it can be unit-tested with a fake session + fake runner (no Postgres, no
Snowflake); real-DB integration coverage is a Week 8 item.

The target lives on the suite (``Suite.target``, resolved by
``run_target.resolve_target``): a targetless suite drives the run to ``failed``
with a clear log rather than running against an unknown table.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from datetime import UTC, datetime, timedelta

from sqlalchemy import select
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from backend.app.core.logging import get_logger
from backend.app.core.secrets import SecretStore, get_secret_store
from backend.app.datasources.registry import build_check_runner
from backend.app.db.models import ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS, Check, Connection, Run, Suite
from backend.app.db.session import get_session
from backend.app.orchestration.registry import get_orchestration_provider
from backend.app.services import orchestration_service, run_service, run_target
from backend.app.worker.celery_app import celery_app

# Polling fallback (#171): look back slightly further than the 10-min beat
# interval so a run can't slip through the gap between consecutive polls.
_POLL_LOOKBACK = timedelta(minutes=15)

log = get_logger(__name__)


def _run_suite(session: Session, *, run_id: uuid.UUID) -> str:
    """Load the run's graph, resolve its target, build the runner, execute.

    The suite's datasource-shaped ``target`` (#215) resolves to the runner's
    ``(table, schema, catalog)`` via ``run_target.resolve_target``; dispatch by
    ``connection.type`` through the runner registry gives a Snowflake / Unity
    Catalog / flat-file suite its correct `CheckRunner` (#146).

    Failures while loading, resolving the target (targetless or malformed suite),
    or building the runner (missing rows, bad connection config, unresolved
    secret) drive the run to ``failed`` so it never lingers in ``queued``;
    execution failures are handled inside ``execute_run``.
    """
    run = session.get(Run, run_id)
    if run is None:
        log.error("run_suite_run_not_found", run_id=str(run_id))
        return "not_found"

    try:
        suite = session.get(Suite, run.suite_id)
        connection = session.get(Connection, suite.connection_id) if suite is not None else None
        if suite is None or connection is None:
            raise RuntimeError("suite or connection not found for run")
        target = run_target.resolve_target(connection.type, suite.target)
        checks = list(session.scalars(select(Check).where(Check.suite_id == suite.id)))
        runner = build_check_runner(
            conn_type=connection.type,
            config=connection.config,
            secret_ref=connection.secret_ref,
            secret_store=get_secret_store(),
            catalog=target.catalog,
        )
    except Exception:
        run.status = "failed"
        run.started_at = run.started_at or datetime.now(UTC)
        run.finished_at = datetime.now(UTC)
        session.commit()
        log.exception("run_suite_setup_failed", run_id=str(run_id))
        return "failed"

    run_service.execute_run(
        session, run=run, checks=checks, runner=runner, table=target.table, schema=target.schema
    )
    return str(run.status)


@celery_app.task(name="run_suite")  # type: ignore[untyped-decorator]  # celery task decorator is unannotated
def run_suite(run_id: str) -> str:
    """Worker entry point. ``run_id`` is a string so it serialises over JSON.

    The target is resolved from the suite (``Suite.target``), so the only
    argument the dispatcher supplies is the run id.
    """
    session = get_session()
    try:
        return _run_suite(session, run_id=uuid.UUID(run_id))
    finally:
        session.close()


def _poll_orchestration_runs(
    session: Session, *, secret_store: SecretStore, now: datetime | None = None
) -> dict[str, int]:
    """Poll every orchestrator connection for recent succeeded runs (#171, ADR 0004).

    The polling fallback for runs that never produced a webhook: iterate each
    ADF / Airflow connection, ask the provider's `list_recent_runs` for runs
    updated within the lookback window, and hand them to `ingest_polled_runs`
    (upsert + trigger-on-success). Goes through the `OrchestrationProvider` seam —
    no per-provider branching. Each connection is isolated: a transport/auth
    failure logs + continues so one bad connection can't starve the rest.
    """
    since = (now or datetime.now(UTC)) - _POLL_LOOKBACK
    summary = {"connections": 0, "recorded": 0, "triggered": 0, "skipped": 0, "errors": 0}
    connections = list(
        session.scalars(
            select(Connection).where(
                Connection.type.in_(ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS),
                Connection.secret_ref.isnot(None),
            )
        )
    )
    for connection in connections:
        if not connection.secret_ref:  # defensive; the query already filters
            continue
        try:
            provider_impl = get_orchestration_provider(connection.type)
            secret = secret_store.get(connection.secret_ref)
            updates = provider_impl.list_recent_runs(dict(connection.config), secret, since)
            result = orchestration_service.ingest_polled_runs(
                session,
                provider_impl=provider_impl,
                connection=connection,
                updates=updates,
                skip_updated_since=since,
            )
            summary["connections"] += 1
            summary["recorded"] += len(result.pipeline_runs)
            summary["triggered"] += len(result.triggered_runs)
            summary["skipped"] += result.skipped
        except Exception:
            summary["errors"] += 1
            session.rollback()
            log.exception(
                "orchestration_poll_failed",
                connection_id=str(connection.id),
                provider=connection.type,
            )
    log.info("orchestration_poll_completed", **summary)
    return summary


@celery_app.task(name="poll_orchestration_runs")  # type: ignore[untyped-decorator]  # celery task decorator is unannotated
def poll_orchestration_runs() -> dict[str, int]:
    """Celery-beat entry point — the 10-min orchestration polling fallback."""
    session = get_session()
    try:
        return _poll_orchestration_runs(session, secret_store=get_secret_store())
    finally:
        session.close()

"""Execute a suite's checks via a datasource adapter and persist the results.

This is the GX-agnostic core of a run: it drives the `Run` lifecycle, calls a
`CheckRunner` (injected — Snowflake in production, a fake in tests), and maps the
returned `SuiteOutcome` onto `Result` rows. GX/Snowflake specifics live behind
the adapter; this layer only knows the DTOs in ``datasources.base``.

Run.status describes *execution*, not data quality: a run that completes is
``succeeded`` even when checks fail (the failures live in ``Result.status`` /
``SuiteOutcome.success``). ``failed`` means the run could not execute — the
adapter raised (e.g. could not reach the warehouse).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from datetime import UTC, datetime

from sqlalchemy import select
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from backend.app.core.jsonsafe import sanitize_json
from backend.app.core.logging import get_logger
from backend.app.datasources.base import CheckOutcome, CheckRunner, CheckSpec
from backend.app.db.models import Check, Result, Run
from backend.app.services import suite_service
from backend.app.services.severity import derive_status, extract_metric

log = get_logger(__name__)


def _now() -> datetime:
    return datetime.now(UTC)


def _build_result(run_id: uuid.UUID, check: Check, outcome: CheckOutcome) -> Result:
    """Map a check + its GX outcome to a `Result`, deriving the severity tier.

    The unexpected-percent badness scalar is extracted once and used both to band
    the tier (ADR 0005 / 0016) and to persist as the durable, SQL-aggregatable
    `metric_value` (ADR 0012). `duration_ms` stays NULL in v1 — per-check timing
    isn't separable from GX's single suite-level `validate()` (reserved seam).
    """
    metric = extract_metric(outcome)
    status = derive_status(
        success=outcome.success,
        metric_value=metric,
        warn_threshold=check.warn_threshold,
        fail_threshold=check.fail_threshold,
        critical_threshold=check.critical_threshold,
    )
    return Result(
        run_id=run_id,
        check_id=check.id,
        status=status,
        metric_value=metric,
        observed_value=sanitize_json(outcome.observed_value),
        expected_value=sanitize_json(outcome.expected_value),
        sample_failures=sanitize_json(outcome.sample_failures),
    )


_EXPECTATION_KIND = "expectation"


def _specs_for_checks(checks: list[Check]) -> list[CheckSpec]:
    """Dispatch checks by `check.kind` to their runner input (ADR 0012).

    v1 implements only the `expectation` kind (the GX `CheckRunner`). The other
    reserved kinds (`freshness`/`volume`/`schema_drift`/`anomaly`/`comparison`)
    are constraint-valid but have no runner yet, so a run containing one raises
    `NotImplementedError` rather than silently feeding it to GX as an
    expectation. This dispatch composes with the connection-type `CheckRunner`
    selection (Week 5, ADR 0011): `kind` chooses the *monitor*, `connection.type`
    chooses the *adapter*.
    """
    unsupported = sorted({c.kind for c in checks if c.kind != _EXPECTATION_KIND})
    if unsupported:
        raise NotImplementedError(
            f"no run path for check kind(s) {', '.join(unsupported)}; "
            f"only {_EXPECTATION_KIND!r} is implemented in v1"
        )
    return [CheckSpec(expectation_type=c.expectation_type, kwargs=dict(c.config)) for c in checks]


def execute_run(
    session: Session,
    *,
    run: Run,
    checks: list[Check],
    runner: CheckRunner,
    table: str,
    schema: str | None = None,
) -> Run:
    """Run ``checks`` against ``table`` via ``runner`` and persist the outcome.

    ``run`` must already be persisted (it carries the id the results link to).
    Returns the same `Run`, updated to ``succeeded`` or ``failed``.
    """
    run.status = "running"
    run.started_at = _now()
    session.commit()
    log.info(
        "run_started",
        run_id=str(run.id),
        suite_id=str(run.suite_id),
        n_checks=len(checks),
        table=table,
    )

    # Everything from here — dispatching by kind, running the adapter, building
    # rows, and persisting them — is guarded so any failure drives the run to a
    # terminal 'failed' state. Without this, a DB error during add_all/commit (or
    # an unrunnable check kind) would leave the run stuck in 'running' forever.
    # rollback() discards any partial result inserts before we record the failure.
    try:
        specs = _specs_for_checks(checks)
        outcome = runner.run_checks(table=table, schema=schema, checks=specs)
        rows = [
            _build_result(run.id, check, check_outcome)
            for check, check_outcome in zip(checks, outcome.checks, strict=True)
        ]
        session.add_all(rows)
        run.status = "succeeded"
        run.finished_at = _now()
        session.commit()
    except Exception:
        session.rollback()
        run.status = "failed"
        run.finished_at = _now()
        session.commit()
        log.exception("run_failed", run_id=str(run.id), table=table)
        return run

    log.info(
        "run_completed",
        run_id=str(run.id),
        suite_success=outcome.success,
        n_results=len(rows),
    )
    return run


# ── read model (PR-C0b: the runs/results surface) ────────────────────────────
# Reads are scoped to suites the user can access — owned (`created_by`) or shared
# (`shares`), the same visibility `suite_service.list_suites` enforces. The API
# layer additionally calls `require_permission` for single-suite / single-run
# lookups (404 hides existence); this subquery is the defence-in-depth filter so
# a list query can never leak a run from a suite the caller can't see.


def list_runs(
    session: Session,
    *,
    user_id: uuid.UUID,
    suite_id: uuid.UUID | None = None,
    status: str | None = None,
    limit: int = 50,
) -> list[Run]:
    """Runs for suites the user can access, newest first (`created_at` desc).

    Optionally narrowed to one ``suite_id`` and/or a ``status``. The accessible
    subquery is always applied, so passing a ``suite_id`` the user can't see
    yields an empty list (the API layer 404s that case up front via
    `require_permission`, but the filter keeps the service safe on its own).
    """
    accessible = suite_service.accessible_suite_ids(user_id)
    stmt = (
        select(Run).where(Run.suite_id.in_(accessible)).order_by(Run.created_at.desc()).limit(limit)
    )
    if suite_id is not None:
        stmt = stmt.where(Run.suite_id == suite_id)
    if status is not None:
        stmt = stmt.where(Run.status == status)
    return list(session.scalars(stmt))


def get_run(session: Session, run_id: uuid.UUID) -> Run | None:
    """Fetch a run by id (no authz — the API layer gates on the run's suite)."""
    return session.get(Run, run_id)


def list_results(session: Session, run_id: uuid.UUID) -> list[Result]:
    """The result rows for a run, in stable check order (`created_at`)."""
    return list(
        session.scalars(select(Result).where(Result.run_id == run_id).order_by(Result.created_at))
    )

1	"""Dispatch a persisted `Run` to the Celery worker — the one place that publishes.
2	
3	Publishes ``run_suite`` **by name** via ``celery_app.send_task`` rather than
4	importing the task object. That decoupling is deliberate: ``worker.tasks``
5	imports service modules (e.g. ``orchestration_service``), so a service importing
6	``worker.tasks`` back would be a cyclic import (CodeQL `py/cyclic-import`). By
7	name there is no import edge service → worker, and the broker resolves the task
8	on the worker side. The ``before_task_publish`` signal still fires, so the
9	``request_id`` correlation header is carried exactly as for ``.delay``.
10	
11	Raises on a broker/publish failure; the caller owns the policy for a stuck run
12	(the probe endpoint surfaces 503; the pipeline-trigger path marks the run
13	``failed`` so it isn't left ``queued``).
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import uuid
19	
20	from backend.app.worker.celery_app import celery_app
21	
22	_RUN_SUITE_TASK = "run_suite"
23	
24	
25	def dispatch_run(run_id: uuid.UUID) -> None:
26	    """Publish the ``run_suite`` task for ``run_id``. Raises if the broker is down."""
27	    celery_app.send_task(_RUN_SUITE_TASK, args=[str(run_id)])

"""Dispatch a persisted `Run` to the Celery worker — the one place that publishes.

Publishes ``run_suite`` **by name** via ``celery_app.send_task`` rather than
importing the task object. That decoupling is deliberate: ``worker.tasks``
imports service modules (e.g. ``orchestration_service``), so a service importing
``worker.tasks`` back would be a cyclic import (CodeQL `py/cyclic-import`). By
name there is no import edge service → worker, and the broker resolves the task
on the worker side. The ``before_task_publish`` signal still fires, so the
``request_id`` correlation header is carried exactly as for ``.delay``.

Raises on a broker/publish failure; the caller owns the policy for a stuck run
(the probe endpoint surfaces 503; the pipeline-trigger path marks the run
``failed`` so it isn't left ``queued``).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid

from backend.app.worker.celery_app import celery_app

_RUN_SUITE_TASK = "run_suite"


def dispatch_run(run_id: uuid.UUID) -> None:
    """Publish the ``run_suite`` task for ``run_id``. Raises if the broker is down."""
    celery_app.send_task(_RUN_SUITE_TASK, args=[str(run_id)])

1	"""Resolve a suite's datasource-shaped target to the runner's (table, schema, catalog).
2	
3	A suite's `target` (#215) is a small JSONB document shaped like the column
4	profiler request (``table`` / ``schema`` / ``catalog`` / ``path`` /
5	``file_format``), datasource-typed. The `CheckRunner` interface is *table-shaped*
6	— for a flat-file datasource the file path rides the ``table`` argument
7	(``flatfile.py``) — so every datasource resolves to the same triple the worker
8	hands to ``run_service.execute_run`` and ``build_check_runner``:
9	
10	    snowflake      → table (+ schema)
11	    unity_catalog  → table (+ schema) + catalog        (catalog.schema.table)
12	    adls_gen2 / s3 → path  (carried as `table`; schema/catalog unused)
13	
14	`resolve_target` is the run-time path (raises if a required field is missing).
15	`validate_target` is the write-time path (same rules; used by `suite_service`
16	when a target is set on a suite, so a malformed/wrong-datasource target is a
17	clean 422 at save rather than a failed run later).
18	
19	FastAPI-free: takes a connection type + the stored dict, raises `DataQError`.
20	"""
21	
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	from dataclasses import dataclass
25	from typing import Any
26	
27	from backend.app.core.errors import DataQError
28	
29	_FLATFILE_TYPES = {"adls_gen2", "s3"}
30	
31	
32	class SuiteTargetInvalidError(DataQError):
33	    status_code = 422
34	    code = "suite_target_invalid"
35	
36	
37	@dataclass(frozen=True)
38	class ResolvedTarget:
39	    """The runner inputs a suite resolves to. ``table`` carries the file path for
40	    flat-file datasources; ``catalog`` is set only for Unity Catalog."""
41	
42	    table: str
43	    schema: str | None
44	    catalog: str | None
45	
46	
47	def resolve_target(conn_type: str, target: dict[str, Any] | None) -> ResolvedTarget:
48	    """Resolve ``target`` for a ``conn_type`` connection, or raise (422).
49	
50	    Raises `SuiteTargetInvalidError` if the suite is targetless, the target is
51	    missing the field its datasource requires (`path` for flat files, `table`
52	    for SQL, `catalog` for Unity Catalog), or the connection type has no run path
53	    (orchestration providers — they are never suite datasources).
54	    """
55	    if not target:
56	        raise SuiteTargetInvalidError(
57	            "suite has no target configured", detail={"connection_type": conn_type}
58	        )
59	
60	    if conn_type in _FLATFILE_TYPES:
61	        path = _require(target, "path", conn_type)
62	        return ResolvedTarget(table=path, schema=None, catalog=None)
63	
64	    if conn_type == "snowflake":
65	        table = _require(target, "table", conn_type)
66	        return ResolvedTarget(table=table, schema=_str_or_none(target.get("schema")), catalog=None)
67	
68	    if conn_type == "unity_catalog":
69	        table = _require(target, "table", conn_type)
70	        catalog = _require(target, "catalog", conn_type)
71	        return ResolvedTarget(
72	            table=table, schema=_str_or_none(target.get("schema")), catalog=catalog
73	        )
74	
75	    raise SuiteTargetInvalidError(
76	        f"connection type {conn_type!r} has no run path (not a datasource)",
77	        detail={"connection_type": conn_type},
78	    )
79	
80	
81	def validate_target(conn_type: str, target: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
82	    """Write-time guard: a non-null target must resolve for its datasource.
83	
84	    Reuses `resolve_target`'s rules so a target saved on a suite is always
85	    runnable. Callers only invoke this when a target is *provided* — a suite may
86	    be created/updated targetless (NULL), which is valid-but-not-yet-runnable.
87	    """
88	    resolve_target(conn_type, target)
89	
90	
91	def _require(target: dict[str, Any], field: str, conn_type: str) -> str:
92	    value = target.get(field)
93	    if not isinstance(value, str) or not value.strip():
94	        raise SuiteTargetInvalidError(
95	            f"target for a {conn_type!r} suite requires a non-empty {field!r}",
96	            detail={"connection_type": conn_type, "missing": field},
97	        )
98	    return value
99	
100	
101	def _str_or_none(value: Any) -> str | None:
102	    return value if isinstance(value, str) and value.strip() else None
103
